channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why CIOs should embrace the potential of data and analytics enablement platforms for a brighter future
item.description: Technology leaders want to harness the power of their data to gain intelligence about what their customers want and how they want it. This is why the overall data and analytics (D&A) market is projected to grow astoundingly and expected to jump to $279.3 billion by 2030.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bumble, Grindr, and Match have agreed to Australia’s online dating code of conduct
item.description: A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday. Bumble, Grindr and Match Group Inc.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 4 in 10 Americans think generative AI will reduce jobs: Report
item.description: Nearly 4 in 10 Americans in a new analysis believe generative artificial intelligence (AI) could diminish the number of available jobs as the technology advances.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Yelp has quietly become a home services powerhouse
item.description: When the COVID-19 pandemic abruptly shut down normal life more than four years ago, few tech companies endured more wrenching change than Yelp.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Fix for BGP routing insecurity ‘plagued by software vulnerabilities’ of its own, researchers find
item.description: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) is not yet the simple fix for the security weaknesses of the internet’s Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) many in the communications industry think it is, a team of German researchers has warned.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How a TikTok trend to help moms became a money-grab opportunity
item.description: A sparkly silver purse, tucked between a bottle of baby lotion and Infants’ Tylenol at a retailer, sparked a recent viral trend. Denaesha Gonzalez noticed the purse (with the price tag still dangling) in the baby product aisle while out shopping.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: What to expect from Apple this October and beyond
item.description: The Apple product machine continues to whirr, with a host of new products, including Macs, iPads, and iPhone SE, expected to appear this fall.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk sending Starlink terminals to storm-ravaged North Carolina
item.description: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk said Tuesday the aerospace company is sending more Starlink terminals to North Carolina following the widespread devastation of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newsom throws AI regulation fight into uncertainty with veto
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom's (D) decision to veto a sweeping artificial intelligence (AI) bill has renewed the debate over the future of regulation of the technology, leaving different sectors of the tech industry largely in disagreement on the best path forward.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google just made a major ChromeOS misstep
item.description: Did you ever watch the early 2000s TV series Scrubs? The show revolved around a young doctor named JD who always meant well but often got lost in his own head and ended up making ill-advised decisions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The role of IoT in shaping smart cities
item.description: As people increasingly migrate from rural areas to cities for better economic opportunities and improved living standards, modern municipalities are faced with challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI 투자 수익 창출? 전문가들이 말하는 ‘AI ROI에의 섣부른 집착이 위험한 이유’
item.description: IT 연구 및 자문 기업 포레스터(Forrester)에 따르면 많은 기업이 AI 프로젝트 시작 후 투자 수익(ROI)를 얻는 데 어려움을 겪고 있지만, 너무 빨리 높은 성과를 요구하면 위험할 수 있는 것으로 나타났다. 포레스터는 일부 IT 리더들이 빠른 가치 창출을 기대하기보다는 실험에 더 많은 시간을 주는 것이 중요하다는 점을 깨닫기 시작하면서, ROI에 집착하는 기업이 조기에 규모를 축소하게 될 수 있다고 예측했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft releases official OpenAI library for .NET
item.description: Microsoft has released its official OpenAI library for .NET, with the goal of ensuring a smooth, reliable integration experience for developers working with OpenAI and Azure OpenAI services in .NET applications. Announced October 1, the now-stable library follows a beta release published in June.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk: Voting for Trump 'only way' to 'save' democracy
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that former President Trump must win the election in November if America wants to preserve its democracy. “Very few Americans realize that, if Trump is NOT elected, this will be the last election,” Musk posted on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Crypto owners evenly split between Harris, Trump: Poll
item.description: Americans who own cryptocurrency support Vice President Harris and former President Trump in equal numbers, according to new Morning Consult polling conducted on behalf of crypto exchange Coinbase.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: San Francisco rolls out AI-powered cameras to combat crime
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses San Francisco deploying three advanced mobile surveillance cameras to deter crime in high-risk areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: An 81-year-old Montana has been sentenced to six months in federal prison and ordered to pay $24,000 in penalties for using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to illegally create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunts in Texas and Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Amy Beth Hanson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO
item.description: Christopher Skaluba, the executive director in the DoD CDAO, said the office is creating tools to provide data-driven insights on AI workforce needs. The post DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Security, AIOps top mainframe customer challenges, BMC says
item.description: Compliance, security, operational analytics, and AIOps are the top priorities for organizations investing heavily in mainframes, according to the 2024 BMC Mainframe Survey, which is in its 19th year and polled 1,000 customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Observe unveils AI-powered agents to speed troubleshooting
item.description: Cloud observability provider Observe today introduced updates to its product set with Project Voyager, a SaaS offering that now includes an artificial intelligence (AI) investigating tool and capabilities to monitor application performance as well as to query Snowflake data storage services.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Using Instagram's new teen account settings to supervise young users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Worried about your teen's Instagram use? Faced with growing pressure to do more to stop online harm, the social media platform has rolled out a major new update to protect young people and give parents more control.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine shows off capabilities of new drone-dropped, flamethrowing robotic war dogs: video
item.description: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense shared a video of a robotic war dog being used in the field with a soldier, who was able to take advantage of the device's thermal imaging.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
